title: triage-cap gate defect: prose counter counts evidence citations as commitments; correction omits the counting rule + declaration escape; demotion chain breaks on operator resets
description:
POST-MORTEM cycles 448/449 (2026-07-02, ultrathink investigation): both died at the triage-cap gate (stage=enforce) after 2 ignored corrections each. Root cause is a GATE defect chain, not agent misbehavior:

F1 — PROSE COUNTER FALSE POSITIVES (internal/triagecap/floors.go CountCommittedFloors): each ## top_n item w/ floor-word+percent counts ONE FLOOR PER DISTINCT KNOWN PACKAGE NAMED ANYWHERE IN THE ITEM — including EVIDENCE citations. Cycle 449's triage complied semantically with the correction (3 tasks, one package each, exactly 3 explicit floors: core 85.0 / bridge 94.5 / audit 96.0) yet counted 7 because its evidence sentences named other packages/functions with percentages ('core 83.1%', 'matchExhausted 66.7%', 'generateACSVerdict 58.3%'). The pipeline's own eval-quality rules REQUIRE rich numeric evidence — the gate punishes what another gate demands. FIX: scope mentionedPackages to floor-TARGET syntax (package adjacent to the floor-word+percent pattern), or require the declaration companion for floor-bearing items.

F2 — CORRECTION MESSAGE IS UNACTIONABLE (reviewer.go:95): it says 'keep at most N coverage floors' but states NEITHER the counting rule (per-package-mention) NOR the declaration-primary escape. The agent cannot comply with a rule it cannot see; it complied with the natural reading twice and was killed twice. FIX: the corrective must state the counting semantics AND instruct writing triage-decision.json {committed_floors:[...]} (CommittedFloorCount is declaration-primary, floors.go:114).

F3 — NO PRODUCER FOR THE DECLARATION: no triage run has EVER written triage-decision.json (checked cycles 448-450) — the declaration-primary design has no producer. FIX: the triage phase contract/prompt must instruct emitting the companion with committed_floors; add a contract check (WARN when absent on floor-bearing reports).

F4 — DEMOTION CHAIN BREAKS ON RESETS (demotion.go ShouldDemote): requires rejection FailEntries at currentCycle-1 AND -2. Cycle 450 WOULD have demoted (448+449 present) — but an operator SIGINT+`cycle reset --force` sealed 450 without a rejection record, leaving a HOLE: 451 needs 450+449 (450 missing), 452 needs 451+450 — demotion cannot fire until two NEW consecutive failures accumulate, i.e. up to 2 more burned cycles after any operator reset. FIX: ShouldDemote should consider the last two RECORDED rejections with the same template within a small cycle window (e.g. 3), treating reset-sealed cycles as transparent gaps; keep the one-cycle relief semantics.

F5 — OBSERVABILITY: the reject reason shows the count but not WHICH packages the counter attributed — corrections would be self-explanatory if the reason listed them ('counted: core, bridge, audit, acs, llmroute, config, panestream'). FloorDivergenceCorrective exists but is empty when no companion. FIX: always append the counted-package list to the reject reason.

CONSTRAINTS: TDD red-first (goldens: the EXACT cycle-449 triage report must count 3 after the fix, not 7); do not weaken the gate's purpose (cycles 280/282/283 overpacking is the real disease it guards); stage semantics unchanged; apicover -enforce + go test -race clean. Verification: replay cycle-448/449 artifacts (preserved under .evolve/runs/cycle-448, cycle-449) as golden fixtures.
